title: can not run a mysql container
body: ### Issue Description I can not run a mysql container. error is[ chown: changing ownership of '/var/lib/mysql/': Operation not permitted ] ### Steps to reproduce the issue podman run -itd -v /Users/banhongyu/Desktop/docker/mysql:/var/lib/mysql:rw --privileged -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=root --name mysql mysql ### Describe the results you received none ### Describe the results you expected Please tell me how to solve the current problem. ### podman info output ```yaml host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.29.0 cgroupControllers: - cpuset - cpu - io - memory - hugetlb - pids - misc cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.1.7-2.fc37.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.7, commit: ' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 94.42 systemPercent: 2.95 userPercent: 2.63 cpus: 1 distribution: distribution: fedora variant: coreos version: "37" eventLogger: journald hostname: localhost.localdomain idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 6.2.8-200.fc37.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 969146368 memTotal: 2048835584 networkBackend: netavark ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.8.3-2.fc37.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.8.3 commit: 59f2beb7efb0d35611d5818fd0311883676f6f7e rundir: /run/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +LIBKRUN +WASM:wasmedge +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: exists: true path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID rootless: false seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: true serviceIsRemote: true slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: slirp4netns-1.2.0-8.fc37.x86_64 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.2.0 commit: 656041d45cfca7a4176f6b7eed9e4fe6c11e8383 libslirp: 4.7.0 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 4 libseccomp: 2.5.3 swapFree: 0 swapTotal: 0 uptime: 0h 13m 23.00s plugins: authorization: null log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan volume: - local registries: search: - docker.io store: configFile: /usr/share/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 1 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 1 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 106769133568 graphRootUsed: 2925187072 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: xfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "true" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 1 runRoot: /run/containers/storage transientStore: false volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 4.4.2 Built: 1677669779 BuiltTime: Wed Mar 1 19:22:59 2023 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.19.6 Os: linux OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 4.4.2 ``` ### Podman in a container Yes ### Privileged Or Rootless Privileged ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details macos 12.6.3 ### Additional information Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @wiilruo!

What are the permission of `/Users/banhongyu/Desktop/docker/mysql`?  Can other users write to this directory?
I also get the same issue.
can be related to the network file system that doesn't allow chown since the `root` user in the user namespace is an unprivileged user.

This looks like you are using podman machine and mount a host directory from the mac. In this case the fs is limited to only your macos UID so the image cannot chown it to anything else.
You could try to run it with `--userns=keep-id` which should make it run under correct uid so that a chown is not needed.
I also get the same issue.

On macOS 13.3.1 I tried

 ```
$ mkdir datadir
$ podman run \
    -c podman-machine-default \
   --rm \
   -d \
   --userns keep-id:uid=999,gid=999 \
   --name mysql \
   -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=foobar \
   --security-opt label=disable \
   -v ./datadir:/var/lib/mysql \
   docker.io/library/mysql
```

Result: The container started and the files in the data directory are owned by the regular user on the macOS host

```
$ find datadir | wc -l
186
$ find datadir -not -user $(id -un)
$ find datadir -not -group $(id -gn)
```


I haven't tested it anymore but I wrote some instructions
https://github.com/eriksjolund/podman-mysql

Thanks, @eriksjolund!

Closing the issue as your instructions seem to work.

@eriksjolund 

How to pass --userns parameter via docker compose file？

I want to use podman as an alternative to docker compose for local development on macOS.

Or should I change my mind and use named volume instead of mount host path?

My compose file:
```yaml
version: "3"

services:
  db:
    image: mysql:5.7
    restart: no
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: password
      MYSQL_ROOT_HOST: "%"
    # sql_mode: rm ONLY_FULL_GROUP_BY
    command: --sql_mode=STRICT_TRANS_TABLES,NO_ZERO_IN_DATE,NO_ZERO_DATE,ERROR_FOR_DIVISION_BY_ZERO,NO_AUTO_CREATE_USER,NO_ENGINE_SUBSTITUTION
    ports:
      - 3306:3306
    volumes:
      - ./data:/var/lib/mysql
```

Use named volume seems to work fine.
```yaml
services:
  db:
    volumes:
      - mysqldata:/var/lib/mysql

volumes:
  mysqldata:
```

@win5do I used something like this and it worked for the docker compose
```
image: mysql:5.7
userns_mode: "keep-id"
```
